#domain/architecture #domain/agents

## Stateless skill funnel: the transcript is the shared state

When multiple steps run inside one conversation, the transcript itself can carry
state between them — no scratch file, no external store.

- Each step reads what earlier steps wrote *into the conversation* and emits its
  own output back into it; the context window is the working memory.
- Mirrors how stateless LLMs sustain multi-turn context: every turn re-sends the
  prior messages rather than persisting state server-side.
- Tradeoff: zero persistence overhead and no stale-file risk, but state lives
  only as long as the session — nothing survives a fresh conversation.
- Works only when steps are co-located in the same session; cross-session
  hand-off needs real storage.

**From this session:** the `wrap-up → pick-up → recommend` funnel was designed
stateless — topics flow between skills through the transcript, with no scratch
file, because all three run in the same session.

Related: [[Context window as working memory]], [[Conversation history as state carrier]]
Sources: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/conversation-state
